findings.description: Loss of multi-row and multi-column merged cell structure at the bottom right of the table.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the text 'no NC standards for dissolved metals' is contained within a large merged cell spanning the last two columns ('Applicable Water Quality Standard' and 'Basis for Standard') and 18 rows (starting from 'Al - Dissolved Aluminum'). In the HTML, this merged cell is split; the text 'no NC standards for' is duplicated across both columns in the first row of that section, 'dissolved metals' is duplicated across both columns in the second row, and the remaining cells are left empty.

findings.description: Merged cells containing text were split and duplicated across columns and rows instead of using colspan and rowspan.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the text 'no NC standards for dissolved metals' spans the last two columns for the 'Al - Dissolved Aluminum' and 'As Dissolved Arsenic' rows. In the HTML, this text is split and duplicated: the 'Al' row contains 'no NC standards for' twice, and the 'As' row contains 'dissolved metals' twice.
